---
title: "Exercises Chapter 2"
author: "JC"
format: html
editor: visual
---

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(baseballr)
```

2.1)

```{r}
fg_data_2023 <- fg_bat_leaders()
head(fg_data_2023)
```

```{r}
fg_data_2023 |>
  filter(G > 140) |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = Positional, y = WAR)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm")
```

There is a slight upward trend, harder positions are associated with more WAR it is not that strong.

```{r}
fg_data_2023 |>
  filter(G > 140) |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = Offense, y = WAR)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm")
```

Offense has a very strong linear relationship with WAR.

```{r}
fg_data_2023 |>
  filter(G > 140) |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = Defense, y = WAR)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm")
```

Defense has a lesser correlation with WAR.

Takeaway: offense is the main thing that drives war, positional adjustment has a positive correlation but not a strong slope. Good defense is helpful, but less impact than offense.
